title: Clarify Internet Identity-only authentication and document lack of Google login

requirements.summary: Update sign-in UI copy to explicitly state Internet Identity is the only supported authentication method (no Google login).
acceptanceCriteria: In the navbar sign-in button (frontend/src/components/AuthButton.tsx), the unauthenticated label references Internet Identity (e.g., "Sign In (Internet Identity)") or otherwise makes it explicit that the sign-in method is Internet Identity.

In the gated-access sign-in card (frontend/src/routes/RequireAuth.tsx), the descriptive text explicitly says the user will sign in with Internet Identity and does not mention Google login as an available option.
All user-facing text added/changed is in English and consistent across the app.

file_operations.description: Adjust the unauthenticated button label to explicitly reference Internet Identity (e.g., "Sign In (Internet Identity)") while preserving existing Internet Identity login/logout behavior and avoiding changes to immutable auth hook files. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Update the sign-in card copy so it clearly states users will sign in with Internet Identity and does not mention Google login; align button label language with the navbar for consistency. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Add a Help page FAQ entry stating Google login is not supported and Internet Identity is the supported sign-in method.
acceptanceCriteria: Help page (frontend/src/pages/HelpPage.tsx) includes a new FAQ accordion item stating Google login is not available and Internet Identity is the supported sign-in method.
The new FAQ entry is written in clear English and does not instruct users to use any unsupported third-party auth provider.

file_operations.description: Add a new FAQ AccordionItem under the existing FAQ accordion explaining that Google login is not supported and that Internet Identity is the only supported sign-in method, using clear concise English. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
